title: Accessible Reader Surface, Untrusted Content, and Safe Links
description: The UI injects feed HTML, opens deceptive links, depends on card color, loses focus after updates, hides source context, and forces infinite scroll. Learners cumulatively produce keyboard-first content-safe feed reader through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.
Prefer safe text, use a tested allowlist sanitizer only where needed, rewrite links, block active content, and preserve readable structure.
Use one page heading, feed and entry landmarks, descriptive link text, metadata lists, dates, language, and source provenance.
